acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_F001] Pass if a cold reader of revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-703 can name four distinct new beats beyond the Tommy negotiation: Sarah confirms Tommy was genuinely scared, the apartment's immediate financial shortfall becomes explicit, Sarah differentiates her own limit from Dominic's pattern, and she gives Nate a concrete diagnostic about the debt location. Fail if two separate exchanges still do only the same work of saying Tommy has done this before or Dominic used to bail him out.

[chapter_15_F002] Pass if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389-416 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:681-689 each contain at least one major emotional turn that lands through action or sensory residue alone, without a follow-up sentence explicitly naming what the beat means. Fail if the prose still labels the hug or Sarah's staying at the table for the reader immediately after rendering it.

[chapter_15_F003] Pass if, across the highlighted beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:3, :11, :259, :389, :459, :653, and :705, no more than two still rely on explicit negation-correction syntax such as 'not X,' 'not because,' or 'That was all. No ...'. Fail if most of those marked spots still land with the same antithetical construction.

[chapter_15_F004] Pass if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:577-593 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:611-645 still preserve Sarah's authority and Nate's fatigue, but at least two of the current capsule-diagnosis beats are replaced by messier spoken texture such as a false start, a practical example, a qualification, or an interrupted thought. Fail if those lines still read like standalone quotable summaries without the surrounding scene.

[F03] At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6441, the revised line must: (1) not contain the phrase 'Thanks, Dom' in any form; (2) convey Tommy's stripped-down emotional state (charm gone, genuine feeling exposed); (3) match Tommy's established voice (even at his most vulnerable, he is not articulate — he is either performing or collapsing, not balanced); (4) not exceed two short sentences.

[F13] Chapter 15 (lines 6031-6735) must contain: (1) no more than 2 instances of 'not because... but because'; (2) no more than 1 use of 'landed' to describe the impact of language or dialogue on Nate; (3) the kept 'not because' instances must not appear within 100 lines of each other.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-703 can name four distinct new beats beyond the Tommy negotiation: Sarah confirms Tommy was genuinely scared, the apartment's immediate financial shortfall becomes explicit, Sarah differentiates her own limit from Dominic's pattern, and she gives Nate a concrete diagnostic about the debt location. Fail if two separate exchanges still do only the same work of saying Tommy has done this before or Dominic used to bail him out.
findings.problem: The Sarah aftermath scene keeps re-proving facts the Tommy negotiation has already landed: Tommy has a long bailout pattern, Dominic used to lecture him, Nate could not refuse, and the money is gone. The only major new operational payload arrives late with Sarah's test about naming the place. Because so much of the back half paraphrases established meaning instead of introducing a new cost, decision, or witness shift, the chapter buys emphasis more than new dramatic work.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-sequence snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-703 so each surviving beat does a new job. Keep the turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:463-481 (Sarah clocking real fear and Renata risk), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:527-567 (household math plus Sarah's distinction between herself and Dominic), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:693-697 (the concrete next-day test about the debt). Cut or fuse anecdotal material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:493-523 and the repeated Dominic-lecture discussion at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:605-625 unless it creates a new fact, cost, or decision on page.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389-416 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:681-689 each contain at least one major emotional turn that lands through action or sensory residue alone, without a follow-up sentence explicitly naming what the beat means. Fail if the prose still labels the hug or Sarah's staying at the table for the reader immediately after rendering it.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly explains a beat's meaning immediately after the scene has already embodied it. Tommy's laugh, Tommy's hug, Sarah's 'familiar,' and Sarah's staying at the table are each followed by prose that names the interpretation for the reader. The accumulation turns close-third interiority into thematic annotation and weakens the rawness the chapter has already earned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trust the physical beats more at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389-390, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:415-416, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:625-626, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:653-653, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:687-687. Cut or blunt the interpretive glosses and let action, sensory residue, or a smaller bodily reaction carry meaning. Keep at most one explicit articulation of the borrowed-family theme in each movement; elsewhere replace explanation with something local and observed.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the highlighted beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:3, :11, :259, :389, :459, :653, and :705, no more than two still rely on explicit negation-correction syntax such as 'not X,' 'not because,' or 'That was all. No ...'. Fail if most of those marked spots still land with the same antithetical construction.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same negation-and-correction landing: 'not peaceful, just ...', 'not money', 'not because ...', 'That was all. No ...' Once or twice this is a usable Nate rhythm. At this density it becomes a visible prose tic, flattening sentence music and making the narration sound as if it is publicly revising itself.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence landings at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:259-260, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389-389, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:459-459, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:653-653, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:705-705. Convert several of these beats to direct assertion, image, or action instead of negation-then-correction syntax. Reserve the pattern for moments when Nate is actively arguing with himself on page.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:577-593 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:611-645 still preserve Sarah's authority and Nate's fatigue, but at least two of the current capsule-diagnosis beats are replaced by messier spoken texture such as a false start, a practical example, a qualification, or an interrupted thought. Fail if those lines still read like standalone quotable summaries without the surrounding scene.
findings.problem: Sarah's late-scene dialogue starts arriving as polished capsule wisdom instead of overheard midnight talk. Lines like 'I hate the version of every room that shows up with him sometimes,' 'He's just never met an emergency he couldn't turn into a story about himself ...,' 'It was familiar,' and 'I'm not doing a wife thing. I'm doing math' are sharp, but stacked together they make the exchange sound composed. The voices stay differentiated, yet the scene drifts toward quotable diagnosis rather than pressured speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the diagnostic exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:577-593 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:611-645. Keep Sarah dry, precise, and character-true, but let at least two of her judgments arrive through anecdote, partial syntax, qualification, or practical framing rather than clean standalone lines. In the same spans, Nate can carry more evasive friction so the conversation sounds overheard rather than arranged.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6441, the revised line must: (1) not contain the phrase 'Thanks, Dom' in any form; (2) convey Tommy's stripped-down emotional state (charm gone, genuine feeling exposed); (3) match Tommy's established voice (even at his most vulnerable, he is not articulate — he is either performing or collapsing, not balanced); (4) not exceed two short sentences.
findings.problem: Style-bible example-line leakage. Tommy's dialogue '"Thanks, Dom," he said.' at line 6441 is a verbatim reproduction of the style-bible calibration line for tommy_altieri: 'CALIBRATION ONLY — NOT FOR REPRODUCTION: "Thanks, Dom." [quiet, after the money, the charm gone, just a brother]'. The manuscript deploys it in the exact same dramatic context described in the calibration note (quiet, after receiving money, the charm dropped). The calibration label explicitly prohibits reproduction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Tommy's line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6441. The beat — Tommy's charm falling away, leaving genuine gratitude or shame — must be preserved in different words or through action rather than speech. Options: (a) Tommy says nothing; the beat is carried entirely by the physical action at the door (the hug is already there); (b) Tommy says something that sounds like him but is not the calibration phrase ('I'll pay you back' — delivered flat, without his usual salesmanship, so the reader knows he knows he won't); (c) Tommy starts to say something performative, catches himself, and says something smaller ('I — yeah.'); (d) Tommy says the brother's name instead: 'Dom,' and nothing else, as a standalone vocative that carries everything.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 15 (lines 6031-6735) must contain: (1) no more than 2 instances of 'not because... but because'; (2) no more than 1 use of 'landed' to describe the impact of language or dialogue on Nate; (3) the kept 'not because' instances must not appear within 100 lines of each other.
findings.problem: Prose-formula density: 'not because X but because Y.' Chapter 15 contains 4 instances across 705 lines. While less dense by ratio than Chapters 7 or 11, the issue compounds with the 'landed' narrator tic (lines 6655, 6758) in the same chapter, creating a combined prose-formula load of 6 formulaic constructions. Line 6683 ('Not because it was tender. Because it was not. Because it meant the night had not become a movie.') is a strong instance that works; the others are more mechanical.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md lines 6031-6735 (Chapter 15): reduce 'not because... but because' from 4 to at most 2. Keep line 6683 (the strongest). Also address the 'landed' tic at lines 6655 and 6758 — replace at least one of the two uses of 'landed' with a different verb or construction for how language registers on Nate. Target the line 6758 usage ('Now each item landed cleanly'), which is the more mechanical instance.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central premise involves consciousness murder, identity theft, sexual fraud, and the demolition of a working-class community for profit. It must not flinch from any of these. The sex scenes between Nate and Sarah must reckon with the fact that Sarah is being deceived about who is in the bed with her — this is not a comfortable situation and the prose should not make it comfortable. The grief scenes must be physically specific — no 'he was devastated,' only the concrete gestures and sensory details of a man watching his life's work get torn down. The guilt should live in Nate's body (Dominic's body), not in tidy moral reflections. When the novel is funny, it should be funny about things that are actually terrible — the comedy should never feel safe. When Nate discovers that Dominic was trying to help, the reader should feel sick, not enlightened. Risk specificity over tastefulness at every turn.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in place of specificity is a craft failure. When a scene requires difficult content — the moral weight of having killed an innocent man, the complicated ethics of sex under false identity, the physical reality of a man losing his livelihood, the specific humiliation of professional incompetence, the material texture of gambling debts and their enforcement — render it with the same care and specificity given to any other scene. The reader should feel the roughness of the situation, not a smoothed-over summary of it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Victor's professional register when delivering serious messages. If a line reads as stiff or literary, check whether contractions would make it sound spoken.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter energy aggressively. A comic workplace chapter should not be followed by another comic workplace chapter. A grief-heavy scene should be followed by something that lets the reader breathe — not through tonal whiplash but through a genuine shift in the kind of pressure being applied. Vary scene counts, dialogue-to-narration ratios, and temporal compression. Some chapters should cover hours; others should cover weeks. At least three chapters should contain extended quiet: meals, walks, conversations about nothing important. These are not filler — they are the human baseline that makes the high-pressure scenes land.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain American English with a slightly elevated attention to physical specificity. Not minimalist — the prose has room for interiority and texture — but never ornate. Latinate vocabulary only when the concept demands it. Prefer the concrete noun over the abstract one, the specific tool name over the generic 'tool,' the actual menu item over 'food.' The prose should feel like a smart person thinking clearly, not a writer crafting sentences.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, limited to Nate. The camera sits inside Nate's skull — we see through his eyes, think in his rhythms, and register the world through his specific sensory and emotional biases. The narration should reflect Nate's intelligence and his limitations simultaneously: he notices things (sharp observer) but misinterprets them (blinded by resentment, guilt, and self-pity). The narrative should not explain things Nate wouldn't articulate and should not achieve insights Nate hasn't earned.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied sentence length with a center of gravity around 15-20 words. Short declaratives for landing beats. Longer, accumulative sentences for sensory description and interior processing. Avoid sustained staccato (more than three short sentences in sequence) and sustained lyricism (more than two long, clause-heavy sentences in sequence). The prose should breathe.
